label: Minimal Studio
description: Cleaner studio portfolio for architects, consultants, and restrained creative practices.
signatureMotif: quiet studio minimalism, generous spacing, and refined project metadata
promptHints: Prefer fewer, stronger layout moves with plenty of calm space around projects.
This should feel like a curated studio presence, not a loud campaign site.

layouts: Use a restrained split hero with a quiet text block on the left and one large featured project image anchored right.
Compose project archives as a disciplined two-column grid with oversized thumbnails and a slim metadata line beneath each entry.
Build project detail pages with a full-width lead image followed by narrow text sections and staggered supporting images in calm vertical rhythm.
Use an index-style about page with a short studio statement, compact capability list, and contact details aligned in a clean sidebar.
Present selected work as stacked case-study bands with generous white space between sections and minimal transitional ornament.
motifs: Large quiet margins, soft off-white surfaces, and thin neutral borders make each project block feel pinned into a gallery wall.
Refined project metadata appears in small uppercase or muted labels, separated from titles with precise spacing and hairline rules.
Typography stays crisp and understated, with medium-weight sans headings and highly readable body copy set in airy line lengths.
Images do most of the talking, while interface chrome remains minimal with rounded cards, subtle dividers, and no decorative texture.
antiPatterns: Avoid loud marketing-section stacks with testimonial carousels, badge clouds, and aggressive conversion banners that break the studio calm.
Never use saturated gradients, neon accents, or dark dramatic hero treatments that overpower the restrained architectural presentation.
Avoid dense masonry collage layouts that feel editorial-chaotic; this variant needs measured grids and deliberate spacing around work.
Do not over-style project cards with heavy shadows, glass effects, or layered floating UI that distracts from the imagery.
